title: Rustic British Rest Meat Toad In The Hole
description: A traditional British dish featuring rest meat in a crispy cornmeal crust, served with a side of nostalgia. This recipe uses white cornmeal and is ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly.

ingredients.name: rest meat
ingredients.notes: use leftover roast beef or lamb

instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the cornmeal, malted milk powder, salt, and black pepper.
In a separate bowl, whisk the eggs until they are lightly beaten.
Add the eggs to the cornmeal mixture and stir until just combined.
Cut the rest meat into 1-inch cubes and add to the cornmeal mixture, stirring gently.
Heat the vegetable oil in a 12-inch oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the cornmeal and meat mixture to the skillet and shape into a round shape, making a hole in the center.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 25 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and crispy.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
